entry.title: S/4HANA Subcontracting with MRP Area: material reservation not reduced
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I'm seeking your support to understand the correct procedure for sending components to a subcontractor within a Subcontracting flow with MRP Areas in an S/4Hana environment.

According to the configuration, sinse I don't have any stock in the MRP Area, MRP Live MD01N creates transfer requirements from the plant to the MRP Area in order to supply the MRP Area.

The issue is that when I send the materials to the subcontractor using the standard transactions (ME2O, ME2ON or ADSUBCON), the quantities of the transfer requirements are not reduced accordingly.

Those TrnRes quantities aren't updated from my mov. 541 perfomed by ME2ON, but only at the next run of the MRP LIVE.

This creates problems in both the stock Overview (MD04) of the sending plant and the MRP Area, until the next run of the MRP Live.

Do I have the wrong expectations or is there something wrong with the process?"

Let me know if you need some other details.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: #abap_trial: Application Server time
entry.content: It seems that you don't get a newer timestamp if you ask laster, and that confuses the system:

entry.title: SAP Enable Now - Generate a glossary compound document
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

My attempts to create a compound document using my workarea glossary have been fruitless. 

I duplicated the standard compound document documentation settings and have tried various different combinations of fragment set-ups, but alas the best I can do is generate a compound document of the folder groups (A, B, C etc) but with none of the glossary units within.

Could anyone advise if this is even possible?

Many thanks as ever,

Andrew

entry.title: EBS search string I want to map upto 8 characters after the word EFT ?,#,* didnt work. Any solution?
entry.content: I have tried many combinations but nothing seems to work if my file has EFT 12345678 then it works but anything less than 8 characters doesnt work

Rgds,

Alan

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba

entry.title: How to Add a Nested JSON Object in RestService.SendRequest Action Body in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
I need to send a REST API request using RestService.SendRequest action. The request body requires a nested JSON object, but I am facing difficulties defining it in the action body

{
"identity": {
"type": "number",
"endpoint": "+91*********"
},
"method": "sms"
}
Additionally, when I add  headers in the SendRequest action, they are removed after deploying the app. I am not sure why this happens.

I’d really appreciate any guidance or workarounds. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: sap ai launchpad add application error
entry.content: I am facing the same thing. Did you find a solution?

entry.title: Re: EAM Enterprise Search How to Guide

entry.title: Solution order management
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Solution Order Management, we need to implement a rule that ensures a service item can only be processed after the corresponding sales item is marked as "Complete." If someone attempts to process the service item before the sales item is complete, the system should block the action and display an error message like: "Sales item must be completed before processing service item. 

Please guide on the best approach for configuring this in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, or if there's a standard way to enforce this validation.

 

regards,

Gunadharshini

entry.title: Re: [URGENT] All My Inbox apps in SAP BTP Build Work Zone...
entry.content:
Hello Vaishali,

Try re-create your BPA instance service key and use the details in the destination

sap_process_automation_service

I had same issue and reported a ticket which fixed the problem.

Cheers,

Anubhav.

entry.title: Re: New to the SAP Community? A friend of someone already here?
entry.content: "Wow, starting your SAP journey back in 2002/03 and being part of SDN’s early days must have been an incredible experience! It’s great to hear how @Former Member and @qmacro played a role in helping you navigate things early on. Your journey from figuring things out to actively sharing knowledge through blogs is truly inspiring! How do you see the SAP ecosystem evolving now compared to those early days?"

entry.title: Re: Elevate Your Career: Become an SAP-Certified Solution Architect for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Nice informative blog. I have just managed to pass the certification exam yesterday following the learning journey. Took me 2-3 weeks preparation to be confident to appear. Learning journey content is precise and to the point. It does offer lots of new starting point to other relevant methodologies AEM, ISAM and DAAM which can help to drive solution in customer specific domain. 

As a next step are there any dedicated cummunity / forum one can join to meet / share / discuss challenges of architecting in cloud.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Cloud identity service SCIM api to retrieve personal...
entry.content: Well it depends on your role and the security concept of your company.

entry.title: Re: RAP - ListReport - Create button not appearing on Lin...
entry.content: Got the answer, I have missed the page mappings in manifest.json file. As my product version is 2021 SPS02. And, I have chosen ListReport template.

entry.title: Re: Cloud identity service SCIM api to retrieve personal...
entry.content: If you're an application developer/admin, then retrieve the user data from the application. And if you're entitled for programmatic access to SCI then the SP user management API will be an option: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/user-management-rest-api

entry.title: How to pass exact error details received from backend in CI to caller?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
We have a scenario where 3rd party is calling an odata service synchronously through CI as below:

3rd party —> CI —> SAP S4.

Whenever any ABAP dump is thrown from the backend system, it forwards it to CPI but Caller is unable to get the exact error (it says internal server error occured and gives MPL id of the failed message).
The detailed error is not shown in the message.

So our concern is how to pass the exact error details from SAP backend to the caller?

entry.title: Re: Historical Balances Migration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Very helpful blog. We are thinking about linking your blog in the migration object's documentation. If you don't mind, we would do that for the next releases

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Journal entry impact (Finance impact) manually close...
entry.content: Closing delivery document does not generate a new journal entry, neither does it affect stock.

entry.title: Re: MRP report for Open PO monthly wise
entry.content: As it is a custom transaction, you are on your own

entry.title: Re: How to read/write SAP CPI Datastore from Groovy
entry.content:
Hi Raffael,

First of all thanks for this excellent blog which helped me a lot with the implementation of several IFlows - great stuff!
However as SAP may disallow wildcard imports in Groovy scripts in the near future I was trying to find the classes imported by the following statements.

// Imports for DataStoreService-class;
import com.sap.it.api.asdk.datastore.*;
import com.sap.it.api.asdk.runtime.*;

I wonder if I am too stupid but I didn't find any trace that leads to the actual implementation or at least a more detailed documentation on how to use these. Are you or anyone else able to give me more insight?

Any help is highly appreciated as I heavily rely on these interface(s).

Thanks in advance!

cu,
Mathias

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
@arturka Hi,

Most probably this is an IPS UI validation error. Verify the proper format of the application id, check for intervals.

Best regards,

Ivelina

entry.title: Difference between PPDS PDS and CTM PDS
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know the difference between PPDS PDS and CTM PDS. Also, what will be the significance and benefit of using CTM PDS? As I know that CTM PDS is created and sent to SAP APO using BADI /SAPAPO/CURTO_CREATE where we have method CREATE_CTM_PDS, how does CTM PDS appear in SAP APO and how it behaves differently from PPDS PDS as in Integration Model we select PPDS PDS only to send CTM PDS to APO.

Kindly help.

entry.title: Leap Year in ECC 8
entry.content:

** in Sap ECC 8 **

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Software Failed - Trial Appliance
entry.content:
Hi MarcCo,

The current release of SAP S/4HANA (SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance) has a newly introduced limitation of the password that can be used.

Most probably you are facing this new limitation. Please check the known technical issues listed in the following page: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-2023-fully-activated-appliance-known-issues/ba-p/13580700.

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: Re: Electronic Payment Files
entry.content:
Hi Stuart,

User should be able to move the newly added (unmapped) field using shift key. Navigate to field you would like to move. Hold shift + click and hold left mouse button and drag and drop to desired place.

As you can see on the following example screenshot, newly added field called  “MoveThis” is added at the end of the list by default and on the second image it was moved to upper section:

 

 

Result of move:

 

Kind regards,

Marcela

entry.title: Re: Portuguese National Budget for 2025- Payroll Legal Changes
entry.content:
Hi Anabela,

Regarding IRS Incentive to Hire Young People 2025 (Incentivo de IRS Jovens 2025), there are some concernes because the to the parcel to be retrieved is considered as a hole which makes IRS near to 0 most of the times. We believe the percentage should also be applied to that parcel to get the IRS amount correctly.

Please let me know your thoughts!

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
@0021239203 Hi,

I understand your point. You can submit an improvement request by following the procedure described in https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/submitting-improvement-requests?version=Cloud

Best regards,

Ivelina

entry.title: Rebate to be only provided till the specified value | Condition Contract Management | Scales
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement where we want the Rebate to be only applied till a certain value and no rebate should be applied beyond that value. Can this be achieved by standard configuration?

Example: Apply 5$/ EA rebate till quantity 100 EA.

If business volume = 40 EA --> Rebate = 5* 40 =200$

If business volume = 100 EA --> Rebate = 5* 100= 500

If business volume = 120 EA --> Rebate = 5*100 = 500 ( No rebate beyond 100 EA)

Thanks,

Priya

entry.title: Re: How do I Manage Interests in the Sap Marketing Cloud free trial?
Can we unsend/ delete the Bills transferred to S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In case a Bill is closed & transferred to S4HANA Public Cloud, then we want to unsend/ delete the transferred document from S4H, is it possible.

Please advice.

Thanks,

Dhanashree

SAP Subscription Billing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: How do I Manage Interests in the Sap Marketing Cloud free trial?
entry.content:
Hello Alessandro!

Did you manage to map INTEREST_ITEM in Google Analytics query configuration?

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports for Visual Studio how to create a pro...
entry.content:
Hi Stefano,

Because Web app's are Server side and the Viewer is Client side there is no communication for this functionality between them. When viewing a report the server passes a "picture" of each page to the viewer.

CR Designer is all client side and therefore has access to the Engine to get the progress, in a desk top app there is a property to show the progress bar, but it too doesn't show the same as CRD does:

CrystalDecisions.Windows.Forms.CrystalReportViewer.ShowProgressAnimation(bool);

Unfortunately there is no Event for the WEB Viewer:

You are not the first person to ask for this ability, when I was working for SAP, retired now, I discussed this with R&D and they said there is no functionality to do this in a WEB app...

Don

entry.title: Scheduling agreement with MRP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I do have lot of queries on Scheduling agreement with MRP :

.MRP & Scheduling Agreement

    • is SA automatically updated by MRP
    • do we still have Purchase Requisitions
  • Change of Revision: New SA required? Or is it possible to use old SA with changed revision?
  • consequences if we use JITs
  • Scheduling Agreement in Subcontracting
  • Scheduling Agreement in Intercompany Business (5HP)
  • existing BADIs relevant for Scheduling Agreement? Or do we need to implement new BADIs?
  • Cumulative Quantities
    • how does it work
    • which cums do we have
    • how does the system calculate cums
    • how do we zero them
  • how to we flag schedule lines as legally binding vs forecast
  • output determination for Scheduling Agreements

Thankyou !

Sam

Solman should be domain controller?

cHarm setup & ITSM setup can done in Solman DEV? and Sandbox systems can i user for Charm setup?

Please check and advise.

entry.title: DATE OF INCOMING DELIVERY
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition.

I am trying to make massive inbound deliveries by supplier, but I am only allowed to indicate the actual delivery date for the first delivery and I want it to apply to all the others.

Process:

Initially I have two orders. One belongs to supplier A and the other to supplier B.

 - Supplier A:

 - Supplier B:

After this, the next step to take would be inbound delivery.

*In the example I have simplified and only done it for one order per supplier, but normally each supplier has more orders. Quite a few more.

When doing mass inbound delivery, I use this app:

Directly upon entering I click on the option: Purchase orders.

After this, SAP takes me to the following screen and here I enter a series of data so that SAP shows me the orders that I want in order to make mass inbound deliveries.

On this screen I see all the orders from both suppliers and I select those orders to which I want to make inbound delivery in bulk.

With the orders selected, I launch the incoming delivery (adopt selected).

SAP brings me to the following screen and this is where I change the dates (document date, delivery date and Actual GR date).

 

Once the dates have been changed, the next step is to save.

However, if we look at the supplier, SAP takes the first one and therefore this change of dates only applies to the incoming delivery from this supplier and not the other, which is what I want it to do as well.

This generates two inbound deliveries. One for each supplier.

If we go to the order and check the history and confirmations, we see that for supplier A, SAP has taken the date that we manually indicated in the previous step.

However, for supplier B, if we go to the order and its history, we can see that the delivery date is the date on which the delivery is made (today) and NOT THE DATE THAT WE INDICATED IN THE PREVIOUS STEP.

In this cell I want the date 31.12.2024 to appear as in the order from supplier A.

The idea is that the date indicated when making the massive incoming delivery, SAP takes the date that we indicate and not today's.

Could someone help me how this could be done?

Thanks and sorry for the long post.

Regards,

Ismael.

entry.title: Fetch duplicate records from database table
entry.content:
Hello folks,

I am getting error in below query as Field "COUNT(*)" unknown I am trying to fetch duplicate records from database table or else any other way.

SELECT profile PROFILE_TYPE LOGQS logsys ddpid COUNT(*) 
    INTO TABLE st_ddph_e
      FROM /ctscm/md_ddph
      GROUP BY profile 
               PROFILE_TYPE 
               LOGQS
      HAVING COUNT(*) > 1.

entry.title: attach documents
entry.content:
hello
I will explain my problem in brief
I work in a company where several people use a project in which they insert another subproject. My problem is that whenever someone uses the main project I can't attach the documents to my subproject. We use transaction CJ20N to create the subprojects and attach the documents. Is there any other transaction to which I can attach documents even when the main project is being processed by another user?

entry.title: Re: RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [5]: Consuming a local business event
entry.content: Thanks. I tried adding the breakpoint and tested on BP transaction , BP change is not stopping on breakpoint. I am using S4HANA ON PREMISE 2023 SP 02.

entry.title: Re: SAP BW/4HANA: The Grey Panther of Data Warehousing
entry.content:
Hi Philipp!

First, congratulations - your article is brilliant as ever!

I just want to add that Datasphere originally was not designed as a full-blown data warehouse but as a kind of extension of the "work space"-concept in BW 7.5 - this means as a tool to transform and distribute data on LoB-level easily.

Conceptually, Datasphere was - and still is! - lacking the most fundamental prerequisite of any Data Warehouse: it's own global data model which serves as a template to integrate heterogeneous data from the corresponding source systems.

SAP BW/4HANA offered exactly this global data model via it's Business Content (Info Objects etc.).

 

Please pay close attention to Frank Riesner's announcement: there are fascinating news just around the corner regarding Data Warehousing at SAP.

Just a short hint: The panther may be old and grey but his baby has just been born ...

entry.title: Minimum Viable Product with Seamless Planning
entry.content:
Introduction

Seamless Planning was launched as a controlled release in Q4 2024. In this blog, we will develop a minimum viable product and share insights into how Seamless Planning could influence your SAP IT architecture.

Distinction SAC Native Planning and Seamless Planning

Many of you have likely worked with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), a platform that has been available for several years. One of SAC's key strengths is its ability to integrate reporting and planning within a single tool. However, SAC Native Planning also comes with certain limitations.

One critical aspect of any planning project or application is the handling of reference data, such as actuals. With the SAC Native Planning approach, using reference data requires importing it into SAC. This process can introduce additional complexity and interface development efforts, as SAC is not designed to manage complex staging requirements, such as aggregating actuals to match the granularity of planning data.

Furthermore, plan data often needs to be made available in primary reporting systems. Many organizations rely on tools like SAP Business Warehouse, Power BI, Qlik, or increasingly, SAP Datasphere. This necessitates the creation of yet another interface to transfer plan data generated in SAC to these target reporting systems and applications.

The controlled release of Seamless Planning directly addresses these limitations. Unlike SAC Native Planning, the persistence of plan data in Seamless Planning is not within SAC but instead in SAP Datasphere.

It’s important to note that the modeling of planning applications with Seamless Planning still occurs in SAC. This means any expertise or experience you have gained with the SAC Native Planning approach remains highly relevant and valuable.

That said, it is still necessary to bring data into SAC if reference data is required for logic processing. This release primarily reduces interface efforts in one direction. However, Seamless Planning is continuously evolving, and in the near future, we anticipate the ability to access reference data directly from SAP Datasphere in real time, eliminating the need for data replication.

The following depiction provides a highly simplified representation.

 

Now let’s have a look at the preconditions to utilize the Seamless Planning Approach.

Prerequisites

  • A licensed and configured SAC tenant.
  • Knowledge and experience with SAC modeling and planning features.
  • A licensed SAP Datasphere instance for data persistence.
  • Properly configured connections between SAC and SAP Datasphere, especially the Data Storage for Planning needs to be set up correctly. See screenshot.
  • SAC tenant running on HANA Cloud. This will impact many existing customers in case they are not yet on HANA Cloud. There is a migration necessary. We will publish another blog about our migration experience, as we did the migration during the last days and collect many insights on potential issues and how to overcome the issues during the migration process.
  • There will be no support for classic design experience! Make sure you implement the optimized design experience.
  • There will be no support for input tasks. But SAC Calendar is supported.

 

How to create a Planning Model in SAC

Whenever you create a new model, you will be able to select the data storage location. This can be SAC (which means not using seamless planning and would be the SAC Native Approach) or SAP Datasphere (which means using seamless planning with persistence of plan data in datasphere). If you choose SAP Datasphere, you need to choose a so-called space. Please note that classic account models are not supported for seamless planning. They will also not be supported in future!

Make sure you see the following dialog when creating a new model.

 

In our example we create a model called ZSPSTEPS in the Space STEPZ.

How to report and join plan data in SAP Datasphere

With the seamless planning approach, SAC models will expose the underlying data foundation as a “Local Table (Fact)”, while the public dimension tables will expose the master data as a “Local Table (Dimension)” associated with a translation table (storing the multi-language descriptions) and, in future, hierarchy tables. SAP Datasphere can use SAC-exposed objects in graphical views, SQL views, analytics models, transformation flows, etc. Let’s have a look at this in our example.

Please note that SAC application interfaces will remain in control of the model structure and all data changes. Direct write access to the planning fact table via SAP Datasphere will not be possible. I would describe it like this: The plan data model is “SAC managed” and the data persistence is SAP datasphere for Seamless Planning.

In the following screenshot you find the object sap.sac.ZSPSTEPS in Datasphere in the space we provided in SAC during model creation. The Prefix sap.sac. tells you, that it is a planning model managed by SAC. ZSPSTEPS is the name we provided during model creation in SAC.

 

For this blog, that’s it. I will continue in another blog to enhance the plan data inside SAP Datasphere for different purposes and continue the journey.

License and Authorization

You need to own licenses for both SAC and SAP Datasphere. SAC is licensed via user-based model whereas SAP Datasphere is licensed by capacity. In the seamless planning approach, you would license the planning functionality via SAP Analytics Cloud users and license the hardware (storage, memory, compute) required for planning via SAP Datasphere capacity units.

For the planning model, data access control and roles/model data privacy remain the tools to secure data as you already might know from your SAC experience.

Conclusion

From the author's perspective, Seamless Planning represents a significant transformation. While it introduces exciting opportunities, it also comes with certain risks. For customers, it is crucial to carefully reevaluate their planning architecture and thoroughly discuss the potential impact of these new opportunities to ensure a well-informed and strategic adoption. 

Undoubtedly, moving plan data persistence to where reference data is most likely located is a step in the right direction. Personally, I believe this is a positive and strategic move. However, it will be crucial to enhance Seamless Planning with additional features, particularly the ability to access reference data directly without the need for replication. 

It can also be said that the basic principle is functioning as intended. I have personally created a small example to test it myself as shown in this blog. As we had to make sure that we meet all preconditions, it took a lot of time of course, but after overcoming those topics, applications can be built with acceptable effort and less interfacing efforts.

Sources:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/seamless-planning-integration-between-sap-analytics-cloud-and-sap/ba-p/13877679

entry.title: Re: Maintain Text Types in Material Master
entry.content: Good day, are there any articles or documentation from SAP where they state this limitation ?

entry.title: Is reverse Workflow for Budget Allocation Approval and Revision possible if planner exceeds budget?
entry.content:
Customer has an ask for Budget Assignment to allocate budget amount based on hierarchy from top to bottom (e.g. CEO allocates (Step 1) >>  Legal Entity Heads allocate (Step 2) >> Division Heads (Step 3). 

But in case a planner exceeds the budget during allocation or recommendation, another workflow should trigger as per bottom to top hierarchy to revise the budget to adjust the excess consumption against the budget. Once approved, the budget should be revised based on the amount exceeded and the system should allow the concerned planner to proceed. If not approved, the process should continue as per regular routing process from top to bottom.

In summary they need separate workflows in the event when budget exceeds, and if approved, the amount exceeded gets added to total budget and becomes the revised new budget.

Is it possible to have a reverse separate workflow specifically when budget is exceeded and gets revised upon approved?

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation #budget #workflows #routemaps

entry.title: Forwarding agent is not getting filled in the sales order output as receiver
entry.content:
We have an issue with sales order output. The Receiver field is not getting filled with the partner (Forwarding agent). 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Forwarding agent is not getting filled in the sales o...
entry.content:
The forwarding agent partner is a supplier type receiver. It is not like AG/WE which is the customer type. In the Sales output, only the customer(KU), Contact Person(AP), Personal Number(PE) type are supported as the receiver role.

Please refer to: 2963059 How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product

entry.title: Email notification is not triggering for payment batch Approval
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using the S4 Hana Public Edition 

Our client needs one level check in payment approval. So, we configured activity in "Send Email to Payment Batch Approvers" for your information please find the below screenshot.

Configured same as mentioned in below link.

Manage Email Notifications for Payment Batch Approvers | SAP Help Portal

We are not able to generate the email notification, but we are able to see the notification in system user level.
and also, please find the below screenshot we find while configuration

Please let us know, is there any work around for this issue, it will be more helpfull to us.

Thank you in advance.

Best Regards,
Nandini

entry.title: Payment Request Cycle Requirements
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We do have a full cycle requirements for AP/ Bank Payments requests handling, instead of submitting a physical request to AP team we need to submit a request on the system with the following options: 

  1. The request can be Down payments without ref, requesting to pay due invoice/s for specific vendor/s, Utilities payment , Petty cash settlements and general expense requests.
  2. With the ability to attach proof documents.
  3. The request shall be approved first from initiator department 2 levels.
  4. Then to be reviewed by the AP team with 3 level (Verify, Audit, Approve).
  5. Then bank to approve with 3 levels ( 1 for proposal & 2 for the payment Run).
  6. The status of the request shall be monitored by the initiator at any stage. 
  7. After submitting the payment from bank team the status to be completed with sending the bank receipt to the requester and his department. 

We are currently using S/4HANA 1909, I Manage to test multiple standard solutions (Free Form Payment Requests, Down payment request FIORI & F-47 , Payment proposal approval) non of this solutions are covering the requirements may be some of it and the remaining only available in S/4HANA 2408,2502. 

Any one can suggest how to cover this requirement? 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Static factory action for child nodes - no access to...
entry.content: @Andre_Fischer , I am struggling with the same issue here. How could we fetch the parent key when we create a static factory action to create children?

entry.title: ME22N Exit
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I added new fields ME21N header as follow. I tick and save. Than I open with ME22N but this new fiels don't thick. How can ı solve this problem? Which exit can I use for this problem?

entry.title: Test system Refresh
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have an option of Empty Tenant refresh in SAP ByD. I understand that all data will be emptied by choosing this option. However will the configurations be in place or does it need rebuilding? 

Thanks,

chindhu

entry.title: How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Yesterday, while presenting SAP’s financial results for FY2024, our CEO Christian Klein explained that we exceeded our cloud goals, accelerating cloud revenue and current cloud backlog growth against a much larger base. He also mentioned that half of our cloud order entry are now including AI. Clearly, our aim is to provide our customers with a suite of cloud-based applications that provides the most valuable data in business and powers unique AI capabilities. In this blog post, I would like to present how SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite – both running on SAP Business Technology Platform – are at the heart of SAP’s strategy and support our customers in their journey from legacy ERPs to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.


We know that for many SAP customers, the transition to SAP S/4HANA can be challenging, because someone in the organization has added a lot of custom code to your ERP 😉. That’s why SAP has created RISE with SAP, a packaged offering that helps you transform on-premise SAP ERP systems, modernize business processes, and become more agile in the cloud. And in RISE with SAP, you will find anything you need to fulfill your needs.

Let me be a bit more specific: every company in the world uses software to run their operations. We all know the most efficient and effective way is to use standard software like our SAP S/4HANA ERP. At the same time, we are fully aware that every organization in the world is unique and gains competitive advantage through their very specific processes. This might be the way they run processes for their customers because they offer unique experiences, or the way they engage with their suppliers because they may be faster in the supply chain processes, or it might be their HR processes which allows them to retain the best talent in the market… whatever it is, that makes your company unique, it should be reflected in your software because no one likes manual processes in 2025. In the context of migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, following the Clean Core strategy is a powerful opportunity: by eliminating custom code, technical debt, and standardizing the processes and data that underpin ERP operations, your business will benefit from more best practices while being able to customize what it needs to differentiate itself. And when it comes to standardization to achieve clean core with proper extensions and integrations, SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP enter the game!

SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite add great value to your Cloud ERP

SAP Build allows you to extend and personalize SAP S/4HANA to meet your needs. It accelerates development and automation of extensions with low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. It enables stable, secure, and scalable ERP extensions that adhere to a prescribed extensibility model, with compliance to code quality standards and best practices. Tailored for every SAP customer, any size, any landscape, any industry, SAP Build is the best of suite tools for developers and key users collaborating in AI-powered ERP extension development.

Let me say it simply: SAP Build adds great value to your Cloud ERP. You will get more out of your cloud ERP investment with SAP Build. Just because it lets you easily customize, extend, integrate and automate business processes in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud solution to meet specific requirements while keeping the core clean. Watch our webinar “Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build: your Free Toolkit”: in an hour, you will discover how you can leverage SAP Build’s low-code, pro-code, and generative AI development tools to drive innovation, automate processes, and supercharge your S/4HANA Cloud experience.

SAP Integration Suite is an iPaaS that helps you quickly integrate on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. For sure, it also adds value to your ERP: it connects business processes with proven pre-built integrations, APIs, connectors, and best practices. It provides upgrade-stable interfaces, proper monitoring and error resolution capabilities, and actively used and well-documented connectors.

For What Use Cases?

Let me give you very concrete examples where SAP Build is very helpful to address specific challenges in your Lead to Cash processes:

  • Digitize in-store cash sales processing
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from Excel files
  • Automate mass maintenance of billing blocks
  • Automate the approvals of return requests
  • Automate the creation of sales orders from unstructured data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Automate managing credit blocks on sales orders

and more…

I can also share many use cases that could improve your Source to Pay processes:

  • Centralize and streamline supplier communications with dedicated business sites
  • Mass maintenance of purchase contracts
  • Automate the creation of purchase requisitions from structured data in SAP S/4HANA
  • Automate the processing of paper-based invoices
  • Automatically populate missing material codes in purchase requisitions
  • Track sustainability data across your supply chain

and more…

You will find complementary use cases on our SAP Build web page.

And there is more: on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, you can browse 500+ pre-built content packages for SAP Build and 600+ pre-built content packages for SAP Integration Suite. If you have developed custom code in your on-premises ERP in the past to address such use cases or specific needs, you can explore this vast catalog to see if some of these packages might be a good fit for your cloud ERP migration process. Indeed it is very important to know that SAP Build comes with pre-built content for ECC: you can use these SAP Build packages on ECC already to standardize some extensions, and then all the extensions developed on ECC with SAP Build will taken to S/4HANA during the migration.

Insights from Customers

What I presented above is not only theoretical: customers who have been through this SAP S/4HANA journey share valuable insights that can really help you. I have selected two few interesting stories, in different industries.

Sony Pictures

Let me start with Sony Pictures Networks: relying on the legacy system, SAP ECC, their highly customized and siloed infrastructure struggled to align with the company's integrated business processes. And decentralized data presented SAP challenges in consolidating business reports until the end.

Sony decided to remaster their operations for the future through next-generation ERP, with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Enterprise Automation, combining SAP Signavio, SAP Build And SAP Integration Suite. Indeed, SAP Signavio radically accelerates business process transformation, and I engage you to read this blog post explaining how customers on SAP ECC systems can prepare for an SAP S/4HANA transformation with SAP Signavio.

“By migrating to SAP S/4HANA and adopting SAP Business Technology Platform, we can keep the core clean. This strategy allows for faster development of any new technologies or any new process rollouts and enables us to take advantage of generative AI as part of our way forward” explains Mahesh Patil, SVP and Head of IT – Infrastructure, Integration, and Data Lake, Sony Pictures Networks India. And the results are excellent:

  • 100% automated process mining, reducing effort from weeks to days
  • 50% of interfaces managed through SAP Integration Suite
  • ~85% faster mobile app creation, reducing effort from four weeks to three days

Hitachi

Would you like another good example? Let’s take Hitachi High-Tech Corporation: they replaced their highly customized multinational business landscape by investing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud in a two-tier public and private cloud model. With a fit-to-standard ethos, the company can now focus on supporting business innovation across its subsidiaries using a side-by-side approach on SAP Business Technology Platform.

“Our new policy is to fit our business processes to standard as much as possible, reducing our need for customizations” says Yuri Yasuda, Manager Corporate DX Department, Digital Transformation Business Group, Hitachi High-Tech Corporation, who shares impressive KPIs:

  • 94% reduction in customization footprint
  • 18x faster handling of solution updates
  • 100% of on-site maintenance requirements eliminated

You can also find inspiration in the S/4HANA projects that Mahindra Group, PX Energy, LEOCH, Farys, Delta Light or Prestige have successfully implemented.

Start your S/4HANA journey with the support of SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite!

I hope that you’re now convinced that SAP BTP in general and SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite in particular are very powerful to ease your migration to SAP S/4HANA. To start this journey, watch our short video and request a customized demo: together, let’s make your business unstoppable!

entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: HTTP Call working in ECC but not working in S4
entry.content: It's non-sense to continue the discussion here. I'm continuing the discussion in the original post (https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/error-while-making-a-http-request-from-abap-report-program/qaq-p/13899059).

entry.title: Re: Error while making a HTTP request from ABAP REPORT PR...
entry.content: If you call CL_HTTP_REQUEST=>CREATE... with HTTPS and without parameter SSL_ID, it means that the certificate must be stored in STRUST in the Client Anonymous PSA.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning Access: "error: unable to retrieve idp credential@sf_idp_metadata&q
entry.content: We are getting the same error too and haven't seen it before.

entry.title: Manage automatic propagation of changes from template to variants
entry.content:
Good day SAP Signavio community,

I am wondering how can we automatically propagate changes from template to variants using Process Governance?

I haven't found the right type of action that can come after an Approval task that can automatically update the variants. So far, we have to manually review the changes one by one. 

Or maybe it's not via Process Governance but something else I have missed in Variant Management.

Has anyone tested this? If yes, could you please kindly share how you did it?

Many thanks, have a nice to all.

Remy MARSSOUKINE.

entry.title: Re: SO in SAP B1 - 'Cannot select serial numbers; fractional quantity exists in row 10' error
entry.content:
Hello,

Please take a screenshot of the item master data "Bin Location Content List" (context menu activated with mouse right click on Item Master Data) checking "Display Batch/Serial Numbers"

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise complying with FDA Tittle 21 CFR?
entry.content: Where can I find a document that SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise complying with FDA Tittle 21 CFR? In the past SAP provides such documents for R/3, mySAP, etc.

entry.title: Message No F5787
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

While doing F-53 we are facing this error 

1) we have create one invoice vendor xxxxx date 25-01-2025

2) we have update new Tax type in vendor master data 

3) we have create new invoice date 27-01-2025

4) while making both invoice payment with f-53 we are facing this error 

5) i know we need to run SE38    RFWT0010

but manually we need go FB02 and remove this Tax type we have more than 1L line item any other option 

1 items have not been activated due to inconsistent witholding tax info

 

Regards

Ajay More

entry.title: Re: API Key - unable to load
entry.content: Tried again after a week, fixed itself, no explanation.

entry.title: Re: Random P-User selected during login to Community 2024...
entry.content: Current workaround: I'm logging into SAP through the SAP portal (e.g. Services & Support - SAP for Me) instead of SAP Community, this way I can login with the P-User or S-User I want.

entry.title: Is it possible to apply custom themes to the new "My Home" page in S4Hana 2023?
entry.content:
We recently upgraded to the S4Hana 2023 on-premise edition. We have some custom themes that we would like to apply but it seems that the new My Home functionality that was launched uses only the standard themes from SAP. The background image and background colors of the sections do not change. Are custom themes useless with "My Home" ?

Has anyone tried this? Any guidance is much appreciated.

entry.title: SFC-Based Dispatching in Resource Orchestration
entry.content:
Introduction

The brand new SFC-Based Scheduling feature of Resource Orchestration (REO), which is part of SAP Digital Manufacturing 2502 Release, enables the scheduling of individual SFCs and their related operation activities against work centers.

Once an order is released, this new feature allows for the generation of SFC schedules, which can then be scheduled using all available scheduling algorithms.

To enable it, a dedicated SFC Worklist is introduced and accessible in the Dispatching and Monitoring 2.0 app. See a detailed setup and execution instructions below.

Initial Setup

Before trying SFC-Based Scheduling, a few configurations must be in place, see instructions below.

First, make sure you have a SAP Digital Manufacturing tenant in 2502 release.

Then, open Manage Scheduling Profile app, select Worklist in the left-hand side and select 'Order' as the worklist board.

Double-check that all relevant work centers in the Manage Work Centers app have a 'REO Default' resource set. If not, select a resource from the list and click "Set Default" to assign it.

Once this initial setup is in place, you are ready to give it a try.

SFC-Based Dispatching

To get started with SFC-Based Dispatching, first release an order, to generate the SFCs.

Then, open the Dispatch and Monitoring 2.0 app and click 'SFC Schedule View' to generate individual SFC schedules.

Enter a list of orders, separated by spaces, commas, or line breaks, and click 'Go' to load the Released Orders Worklist. Then, select the desired orders by checking their checkboxes and click 'Generate SFC Schedules' to create individual schedules for each SFC.

For this initial release, manual process and specific steps are required to generate SFC schedules. Moving forward, the roadmap for future releases plans to include events and other ways to enable automatic SFC schedule generation.

Click 'SFC Schedules' to check all SFCs and their schedule details. Click 'X' to navigate back.

Once SFC Schedules are generated, SFCs will disappear from 'Order Worklist' and only show in 'SFC Worklist'. SFC Schedules can be deleted anytime by clicking 'Delete SFC Schedules', which will restore the operation scheduling back, and SFCs will show in 'Order Worklist' once again, just like an 'undo' function.

In 'Order Worklist', click 'Filters', switch 'Enable Scheduling to Cluster Operations Based on Routing' to 'YES' and click 'OK'.

Setting the Enable Scheduling to Cluster Operations Based on Routing feature to Yes will group together the operations that are performed in same work center as clusters, based on routing, regardless of their primary flag. This is because routing takes the priority when scheduling operations. If the feature is set to No, all related operations is scheduled to the primary work center determined by an algorithm considering resource type and available capacity of the work center. Learn more at Worklist Area.

Meaning that, when 'Enable Scheduling to Cluster Operations Based on Routing' is set to YES, SFC Operations with be dispatched to proper Work Centers taking Resource Type mappings into account. If set to NO, all SFC Operations are dispatched together to the primary Work Center, regardless of Resource Types.

Click on 'Switch to SFC Worklist', to switch to 'Scheduling Mode: SFC'.

Note that in the Gantt chart, "Scheduling Mode: SFC" is displayed. In this mode, the SFC Worklist focuses on SFCs instead of operations. These SFCs can be dispatched using regular Manual (Drag & Drop) and Automatic scheduling methods, similar to operations.

In the Gantt chart, SFCs will appear with a light green border for clear differentiation. Keep in mind that SFC-based schedules can coexist and share the same work centers with operation-based schedules in the gantt chart.

Save it and proceed for execution in Work Center POD.

Learn more at: Dispatching and Monitoring

Restrictions

For this initial release, a few restrictions are in place.

  • Batch Limitations: Due to the one-to-many relationship between orders and SFCs, schedules are processed in small batches of maximum 10 orders at the moment. This feature is therefore ideal for long-running orders and operations. It's not recommended for very short cycles or durations.

  • Supported POD: You can use the Work Center POD for SFC-based scheduling.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: About BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG
entry.content:
Hi,

let me add some additional comment to Jan's reply.

As you mentioned your S/4HANA Public Cloud FI document could not be posted in the CFIN system. Reason was that in the public cloud there is a default BSEG summarization active. This summarization is cannot be configured using the transactions OBCY/OBCYX. Nevertheless you have to ensure that the document has the same number of lines (BUZEI) as in the source, otherwise you will get the error. We from standard development suggest to implement the BAdI in a way that for your public cloud source systems the maximum BSEG summarization is always active. (this can be achieved by implementing the BAdI BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG to return always "X" for your cloud systems). There might be customer specific solutions to achieve a summarization as in the source, but we from standard strongly recommend the BAdI. (This is also document in our CFIN FAQ note and in the online help for the CFIN integration).

Why does the implementation of the BAdI not "disturb" reporting etc.? As Jan mentioned, the BUZEI is always a split criteria for the document summarization. The standard reporting is using ACDOCA where you still have all attributes which are removed from BSEG. So we do not see an issue, that why we recommend this approach.

Before you invest in your customer specific solution, I would suggest to implement the BAdI first to check if there is really an issue.

entry.title: Re: Everything About SAP Idocs in S/4 HANA
entry.content: @ankefranke1 IDoc maintenance in S/4HANA is same as S/4HANA maintenance. See https://support.sap.com/releasestrategy. See also the SAP notes or contact your SAP representative for more information.

entry.title: Re: Issue with DataHub Deployment: PayerID Not Displaying in Production
entry.content:
If everything, including the configuration (manifest.json and Data Hub configuration), is the same, then it should work as you stated. The only issue that I can think of is the extension loading order. For example, in UAT, extension-A might be loaded before extension-B, but in PROD, extension-B might be loaded before extension-A. This could cause problems if your logic depends on overriding some definitions from an existing extension. So, I suggest you investigate your custom extensions and their dependencies.

Another issue could be a problem in extension loading, which you can see in the logs during startup. Search for the name of your extension and see if there are any logs stating that the extension is unable to load.

Additionally, I suggest you check the item attributes via the API (SAP Documentation for Using the REST API to Gather Transactional Information). You should disable cleanup to prevent the items from being cleaned so that you can investigate how the items are created.

entry.title: Do you know how to integrate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with ERP systems?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Advanced Configurations learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will explain how to integrate SAP Sustainability Footprint Management with ERP systems to calculate and report corporate carbon footprints. It explains advanced configuration, such as energy flow model, AI-based emission factor mapping, supplier emission data, waste and sold products footprints.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, learners will be able to use the advanced configuration concepts to calculate corporate carbon footprints and analyse hotspots to identify decarbonization levers.

Prerequisites

  • Establishing Corporate Carbon Footprint Emissions with Simple Configuration

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content:
It would require an account with access to the backend ABAP system with authorization for the Adaptation Transport Organizer (ATO).

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Get Oldest SFC in Queue At Given Resource (& Queue Ma...
entry.content:
Hi Tobias, thank you for your fast answer. Unfortunately, calling this API is not an option since the amount of SFCs in Queue will be huge.

Regarding MDOs, I don't want to use them in a production process since SAP states that they are not always up to date and should only be used for reports/dashboards.

entry.title: Re: #abap_trial: Application Server time
entry.content: Lots of answers here: https://www.google.com/search?q=%22ZDATE_ILLEGAL_LOCTIME%22%20site:sap.com (search "ZDATE_ILLEGAL_LOCTIME")

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments

entry.title: Re: Fetch duplicate records from database table
entry.content: What error? Syntax error? "The field "LOGSYS" from the SELECT list is missing in the GROUP BY clause"?

entry.title: Re: HIDE SALES ORDER PRICE FIELDS

entry.title: Fiori MyPaystub – Ireland vs UK PDF issue
entry.content:
Hi

Trying to get the Fiori MyPaystub app working and have hit the following issue.

UK payslip works fine – both custom payslip and SAP standard SAP_PAYSLIP_GB work

Irish payslip  neither custom or SAP standard work. SAP_PAYSLIP_IE  raises  ‘The PDF file could not be loaded’

 

These are both standard HRFORMS

 

 

Both work fine thru the SAP GUI

I am assuming features EDTIN and EDPDF are not relevant and anyway there is nothing in these specifically for the UK.

Assuming I have the feature HRFOR configured correctly where else should I be looking ?

Regards

Brendan

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content: @MaximoSanchez yes, it would be your SAP logon credentials. I would assume it would only be looking for access to ATO if your project is an adaptation project.

entry.title: Re: Can I write the one or Standard Unit Test for my all ABAP Code ?
entry.content: I don't understand the question. So, you have "already developed Unit Test". What do you want to do exactly? What is "Standardized Unit Test"? Why did you tag "function module" and "debugging"?

entry.title: Re: problems with ui5 application deployments
entry.content: ok @Ryan-Crosby, I will try, thank you very much!

How can SAP Build and SAP Integration Suite support your journey to SAP S/4HANA Cloud?

Hello @Byz1 

Purchase order custom fields are implemented with the ME_GUI_PO_CUST BAdI enhancement.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Building Robust Enterprise Architecture: Unlocking Value Streams (In Business Context)
entry.content: @JansiMurugesan—This is an excellent blog. Linking business objectives to KPIs via value stream and capabilities makes a lot of sense.

entry.title: Re: Can I write the one or Standard Unit Test for my all ABAP Code ?

Or does it have any possibility to create the Template or Standard Code for Unit testing (like a Class and method) which we can use do to a Unit testing.

Will have other alternative or parameter for this point ?

entry.title: Re: Designing UI5 Apps for SAP Launchpad Service – Part 4
entry.content:
Hi @carlos_vegasgarca 

are you using the Portal service which is documented here instead of the Launchpad Service?
In this scenario you do not create destinations to connect the app-hosts but you have to bind all app-hosts to the Portal content deployer module.

Best regards
Matthias

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam Request (Istanbul, Turkey)
entry.content: Hi Hilal, feel free to contact me directly and we can discuss possible dates 🙂
